summary: Extract shared constants module for app-level constants in current use
problem_statement: Hardcoded values (status strings, route paths, configuration defaults, UI labels) are scattered across multiple files. When a value needs to change, every occurrence must be found and updated independently, risking inconsistencies and bugs. Centralising these into a shared constants module makes the codebase easier to maintain and reduces the surface area for errors.
in_scope: Identify hardcoded constants currently duplicated or scattered across the application (status strings, route paths, config defaults, magic numbers, UI labels)
Create a shared constants module exporting these values with descriptive names
Replace inline hardcoded values with references to the shared constants in refactored code paths
Type the constants using const assertions or enums where appropriate
out_of_scope: Introducing a runtime configuration system or environment variable management
Extracting constants from code paths not touched by R2
Creating new constants for values that don't currently exist in the codebase
requirements: A shared constants module exists with typed, named exports for identified app-level constants
Refactored code paths reference the shared constants instead of inline hardcoded values
No runtime behaviour changes result from the extraction
acceptance_criteria: Shared constants module exists and is imported by refactored code paths
No duplicate hardcoded values remain for the extracted constants in refactored paths
npm run check passes with no new type errors introduced
dependencies: P2-R2-05 — type safety work should be complete so constants can use proper types
risks: Over-extraction — not every literal string warrants extraction; focus on values that are genuinely shared or likely to change
implementation_notes: Use 'as const' assertions for string literal unions. Group constants logically (e.g. TASK_STATUS, ROUTES, API_PATHS). Place in a shared/constants or lib/constants module.
